title: Biofluid sensing devices with integrated charge separation buffering
abstract: Devices and methods are described herein for separating electrically charged particles in a biofluid sensing device employing electrochemical aptamer-based ("EAB") sensors to limit ion-induced variability in the sensor outputs. In the disclosed embodiments, charge separation is accomplished with continuous biofluid sample flow in a sensor channel in order to limit ion exposure without decreasing the flow rate of biofluid through the device or increasing the biofluid volume required for sampling. Certain embodiments create an electric field to deflect ions away from sensors. Other embodiments create a magnetic field to accomplish charge separation. Y et other embodiments use a charged polymer membrane to electrostatically draw certain ions away from sensors. A method for reducing EAB sensor variability due to ions is also disclosed that uses such charge separation techniques within a biofluid channel.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A sensing device, comprising: a channel for transporting a biofluid, wherein the biofluid flows through the channel in a flow direction; a sensor for measuring a characteristic of an analyte in the biofluid, the sensor comprising a plurality of sensing elements and a sensor electrode, and wherein the sensor is in fluidic contact with the biofluid; and a force field applied at an angle to the flow direction, wherein the angle is greater than zero degrees and no more than ninety degrees, wherein the field applies a vector to a plurality of ions in the biofluid, and wherein the vector is directed away from the sensor. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the sensor is one of a plurality of sensors. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of ions is a cation. 4. The device of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of ions is an anion. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the sensor is an electrochemical aptamer-based sensor. 6. The device of claim 1, further comprising a first separation electrode and a second separation electrode configured to generate the force field, wherein the first separation electrode has a positive electrical charge and second separation electrode has a negative electrical charge, and wherein the force field is an electric field. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the first separation electrode is arranged proximate to a first side of the channel, and the second separation electrode is arranged proximate to a second side of the channel, and wherein the first side is opposite the second side. 8. The device of claim 6, wherein the angle is ninety degrees to the flow direction. 9. The device of claim 6, wherein the first separation electrode is located at a first distance from the sensor, and the second separation electrode is located at a second distance from the sensor, and wherein the second distance is greater than the first distance. 10. The device of claim 6, wherein the channel further comprises a membrane having a selective permeability. 11. The device of claim 10, further comprising a reservoir containing a buffering solution, wherein the reservoir is in fluidic communication with the channel through the membrane. 12. The device of claim 10, wherein the first and second separation electrodes are integrated with the membrane. 13. The device of claim 1, further comprising a magnet configured to generate the force field, and wherein the force field is a magnetic field. 14. The device of claim 1, further comprising a first conducting coil and a second conducting coil configured to generate the force field, and wherein the force field is a magnetic field. 15. A sensing device, comprising: a channel for transporting a biofluid, wherein the biofluid flows through the channel in a flow direction; a sensor for measuring a characteristic of an analyte in the biofluid, the sensor comprising a plurality of sensing elements and a sensor electrode, and wherein the sensor is in fluidic contact with the biofluid; and a membrane located at a distance from the sensor, wherein the membrane extends across a dimension of the channel perpendicularly to the flow direction, and the membrane comprises one or more polymer coatings configured to electrostatically attract a plurality of ions. 16. The sensing device of claim 14, wherein the membrane comprises a plurality of functional groups for attracting the plurality of ions. 17. A method of reducing ion-induced signal variability in a biosensing device, the method comprising: receiving a biofluid sample containing a concentration of an analyte and a plurality of ions; transporting the biofluid sample through a channel, the biofluid sample fluidically contacting an electrochemical aptamer-based sensor located in the channel; and applying a force field to the biofluid in the channel, wherein the force field directs a subset of the plurality of ions away from the sensor. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the force field is one of an electrical field or a magnetic field. 19. The method of claim 10, wherein the separating step comprises using a polymer surface in fluidic contact with the sample in the channel to electrostatically separate ions in the sample. 20. The method of claim 10, wherein the separating step comprises utilizing a charged polymer matrix on a surface of the channel to electrostatically separate ions in the sample.
